name: First Trust
description_short: This is not a consumer finance company—it's a legal reference article about a 1998 Supreme Court case involving federal credit union membership rules.
description_long: First Trust does not appear to operate as a consumer finance company. The provided URL is a Grokipedia legal encyclopedia entry documenting National Credit Union Administration v. First National Bank & Trust Co., a landmark 1998 Supreme Court decision. The case itself concerns federal credit union membership regulations, not a company offering financial products or services to consumers. The website content is educational and historical in nature, explaining the Supreme Court's ruling that the Federal Credit Union Act requires all members of an occupational federal credit union to share a single common bond, and how Congress subsequently responded with the 1998 Credit Union Membership Access Act. The article traces credit union history back to 1909 and documents the regulatory framework established by the 1934 Federal Credit Union Act. There is no indication that 'First Trust' operates as a consumer-facing financial services company, offers accounts, loans, credit products, or any other direct financial services to consumers. The only institutional references are to 'First National Bank & Trust Co.,' which was a respondent in the lawsuit challenging the NCUA's regulatory interpretation. This appears to be a categorization error or a reference to a legal case rather than an active consumer finance business.
services: Personal loans (typically lower rates than banks)
pros: Not-for-profit — typically lower fees and better rates than banks
NCUA insured — deposits protected up to $250,000
Low-income designated — may offer Payday Alternative Loans (PALs)
cons: This is a legal reference document, not a company profile—no consumer products or services are offered
The URL does not resolve to an operating financial services company
No contact information, business operations, or consumer-facing offerings are described
best_for: Lower fees and better loan rates than banks
diagnosis: This entry should not exist as a company profile on CreditDoc. The website content is a legal encyclopedia article about a Supreme Court case, not a financial services company. A data correction is required to remove this entry or redirect it to factual information about an actual operating company named First Trust.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
